danger_signals.meaning: Don Karnage's faction represents active threat to player objectives
Antagonistic force attempting to monopolize cargo delivery market
Platformer hazards impeding cargo collection objective

safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward level completion (10+ required to unlock exit)
Safe operational base for delivery business activities

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cargo boxes as representation of legitimate commerce

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Air pirates hired as antagonistic force
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Competition for Cape Suzette work contract
Hired antagonists representing threat to player business

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Levels populated with obstacles and enemy air pirates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must collect cargo boxes while avoiding/defeating pirates to secure work contract

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shere Khan's corporate interests opposing Rebecca's business
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gameplay loop structured around competitive cargo delivery with sabotage elements
Combat/avoidance gameplay during cargo collection phases

environmental_storytelling.example: Requirement to collect 10+ cargo boxes communicates player role as delivery service operator defending business
Baloo and Kit as protagonists, Shere Khan as absent but present antagonist force, Rebecca as business owner stakeholder
Competing for lifetime work contract from Cape Suzette mirrors economic struggle between legitimate business and corporate sabotage

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Collect cargo boxes, navigate platforms, avoid or defeat air pirates
Patrol levels as obstacles, engage player during cargo collection
Stakes for player success; represents legitimate economic interest
Indirect opposition through hired pirates; manifests as systemic threat rather than direct encounter

notes: TaleSpin NES game structure emphasizes cargo delivery as core mechanic, with world-state communicated through economic conflict narrative. Visual communication relies on Disney animated series IP recognition for faction/character identity. Limited platform-specific detail in source material prevents detailed visual manifestation data. Game presents two-sided business conflict (Rebecca vs. Shere Khan) with pirates as hired opposition. Genesis/Game Gear versions support two-player cooperative gameplay, reinforcing collective delivery business theme.
